main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct mode_descriptor_t
Metadata for a mode.

Macros


#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'
#define VERSION ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL
#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Typedefs


typedef int(* mode_entry_point_t) (void)

Functions


atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO (g_should_exit)
bool should_exit (void)
void signal_exit (void)
void set_interrupt_callback (void(*cb)(void))
void setup_signal_handlers (void)
int main (int argc, char *argv[])

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

This file implements the main entry point for the unified ascii-chat binary, which provides server, client, mirror, and discovery service functionality in a single executable.

The dispatcher now delegates ALL option parsing (including mode detection and binary-level options) to options_init(), then simply dispatches to the appropriate mode-specific entry point based on the detected mode.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

3.0

Definition in file main.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'

#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \


  if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \


    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \


    _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Definition at line 181 of file main.c.


181 #define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()                                                                                      \
182   do {                                                                                                                 \
183     static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \
184     if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \
185       NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \
186       _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \
187     }                                                                                                                  \
188   } while (0)

atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO

Global flag indicating application should exit (used by all

References g_should_exit.






int
  main (int argc, char * argv[])


Definition at line 463 of file main.c.


463                                  {
464   // Set global argc/argv for early argv inspection (e.g., in terminal.c)
465   g_argc = argc;
466   g_argv = argv;
467 
468   // Validate basic argument structure
469   if (argc < 1 || argv == NULL || argv[0] == NULL) {
470     return 1;
471   }
472 
473   // Show cursor early in case a previous session crashed with it hidden
474   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
475 
476   // Initialize the named registry for debugging (allows --debug-state to show registered synchronization primitives)
477 #ifndef NDEBUG
478   named_init();
479   // Initialize atomic operations debug tracking
480   debug_atomic_init();
481   // Register global atomics with descriptive names for debug sync state monitoring
482   REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS();
483   // Static atomics are now auto-registered via ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO macro at definition time
484   // Register all packet types from the packet_type_t enum
485   named_registry_register_packet_types();
486 #endif
487 
488   // Register the main thread IMMEDIATELY after named_init() to ensure it's available for all subsequent allocations
489 #ifndef NDEBUG
490   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD(asciichat_thread_self(), "main", NULL);
491   // Also save main thread ID for memory reporting (must be very early)
492   debug_sync_set_main_thread_id();
493 #endif
494 
495   // VERY FIRST: Scan for --color BEFORE ANY logging initialization
496   // This sets global flags that persist through cleanup, enabling --color to force colors
497   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
498     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--color=true") == 0) {
499       g_color_flag_passed = true;
500       g_color_flag_value = true;
501       break;
502     } else if (strncmp(argv[i], "--color=", 8) == 0) {
503       g_color_flag_passed = true;
504       g_color_flag_value = strcmp(argv[i], "--color=false") != 0;
505       break;
506     }
507   }
508 
509   // SECOND: Scan for --grep BEFORE ANY logging initialization
510   // This ensures ALL logs (including from shared_init) can be filtered
511   // Supports multiple --grep patterns (ORed together)
512   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
513     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--grep") == 0) {
514       const char *pattern = argv[i + 1];
515       asciichat_error_t filter_result = grep_init(pattern);
516       if (filter_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
517         log_error("Invalid --grep pattern or invalid flags: \"%s\" - use /pattern/flags format (e.g., \"/query/ig\" or \"/literal/F\")", pattern);
518         return 1;
519       }
520       i++; // Skip the pattern argument
521     }
522   }
523 
524   // Detect terminal capabilities early so colored help output works
525   // Logging will be initialized by asciichat_shared_init() before options_init()
526   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
527   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
528   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
529 
530   // Warn if Release build was built from dirty working tree
531 #if ASCII_CHAT_GIT_IS_DIRTY
532   if (strcmp(ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE, "Release") == 0) {
533     // Dirty git warning will be logged after logging initialization
534   }
535 #endif
536 
537   // Load color scheme early (from config files and CLI) before logging initialization
538   // This allows logging to use the correct colors from the start
539   options_colorscheme_init_early(argc, argv);
540 
541   // Parse --color-scheme from argv early to set logging colors for help output
542   const char *colorscheme_name = "pastel"; // default
543   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
544     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color-scheme") == 0) {
545       colorscheme_name = argv[i + 1];
546       break;
547     }
548   }
549 
550   // Load and apply colorscheme to logging BEFORE options_init() so help gets colors
551   color_scheme_t scheme;
552   if (colorscheme_load_builtin(colorscheme_name, &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
553     log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
554   } else {
555     // Fall back to default pastel if scheme not found
556     if (colorscheme_load_builtin("pastel", &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
557       log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
558     }
559   }
560 
561   // Initialize logging colors so they're ready for help output
562   log_init_colors();
563 
564   // EARLY PARSE: Find the mode position (first positional argument)
565   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
566   int mode_position = -1;
567   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
568     if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
569       // Found a positional argument - this is the mode or session string
570       mode_position = i;
571       break;
572     }
573 
574     // Skip option and its argument if needed
575     // Check if this is an option that takes a required argument
576     const char *arg = argv[i];
577     const char *opt_name = arg;
578     if (arg[0] == '-') {
579       opt_name = arg + (arg[1] == '-' ? 2 : 1);
580     }
581 
582     // Handle --option=value format
583     if (strchr(opt_name, '=')) {
584       continue; // Value is part of this arg, no need to skip next
585     }
586 
587     // Options that take required arguments
588     if (strcmp(arg, "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "-L") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-level") == 0 ||
589         strcmp(arg, "--config") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--color-scheme") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-template") == 0) {
590       if (i + 1 < argc) {
591         i++; // Skip the argument value
592       }
593     }
594   }
595 
596   // EARLY PARSE: Determine mode from argv to know if this is client-like mode
597   // The first positional argument (after options) is the mode: client, server, mirror, discovery, acds
598   bool is_client_like_mode = false;
599   if (mode_position > 0) {
600     const char *first_arg = argv[mode_position];
601     if (strcmp(first_arg, "client") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "mirror") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "discovery") == 0) {
602       is_client_like_mode = true;
603     }
604   }
605 
606   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log file from argv (--log-file or -L)
607   // Must appear BEFORE the mode
608   // Generate default log path based on build type and mode
609   char default_log_path[PATH_MAX];
610   asciichat_mode_t detected_mode = MODE_INVALID;
611   if (mode_position > 0) {
612     const char *mode_str = argv[mode_position];
613     // Convert mode string to enum
614     if (strcmp(mode_str, "server") == 0) {
615       detected_mode = MODE_SERVER;
616     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "client") == 0) {
617       detected_mode = MODE_CLIENT;
618     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "mirror") == 0) {
619       detected_mode = MODE_MIRROR;
620     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "acds") == 0 || strcmp(mode_str, "discovery-service") == 0) {
621       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE;
622     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "discovery") == 0) {
623       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
624     }
625   }
626   generate_default_log_path(detected_mode, default_log_path, sizeof(default_log_path));
627 
628   const char *log_file = default_log_path;
629   int max_search = (mode_position > 0) ? mode_position : argc;
630   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
631     if ((strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0)) {
632       log_file = argv[i + 1];
633       break;
634     }
635   }
636 
637   // EARLY PARSE: Check for --json (JSON logging format)
638   // If JSON format is enabled, we'll use the JSON filename during early init
639   // --json MUST appear BEFORE the mode
640   bool early_json_format = false;
641   if (mode_position > 0) {
642     for (int i = 1; i < mode_position; i++) {
643       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
644         early_json_format = true;
645         break;
646       }
647     }
648   } else {
649     // If no mode found, search entire argv
650     for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
651       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
652         early_json_format = true;
653         break;
654       }
655     }
656   }
657 
658   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log template from argv (--log-template)
659   // Note: This is for format templates, different from --log-format which is the output format
660   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
661   const char *early_log_template = NULL;
662   bool early_log_template_console_only = false;
663   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
664     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-template") == 0) {
665       early_log_template = argv[i + 1];
666       break;
667     }
668   }
669   for (int i = 1; i < max_search; i++) {
670     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-format-console-only") == 0) {
671       early_log_template_console_only = true;
672       break;
673     }
674   }
675 
676   // Initialize shared subsystems BEFORE options_init()
677   // This ensures options parsing can use properly configured logging with colors
678   // If JSON format is requested, don't write text logs to file
679   // All logs will be JSON formatted later once options_init() runs
680   const char *early_log_file = early_json_format ? NULL : log_file;
681   asciichat_error_t init_result = asciichat_shared_init(early_log_file, is_client_like_mode);
682   if (init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
683     return init_result;
684   }
685 
686   // Route logs to stderr if stdout is piped (MUST happen early, before options_init logs)
687   // This keeps stdout clean for data output (e.g., --snapshot mode piped to file)
688   if (terminal_should_force_stderr()) {
689     log_set_force_stderr(true);
690   }
691 
692   // Register cleanup of shared subsystems to run on normal exit
693   // Library code doesn't call atexit() - the application is responsible
694   // IMPORTANT: atexit handlers run in LIFO order - last registered runs first
695   // So register order (top to bottom) = execution order (bottom to top):
696   // 1. PCRE2 cleanup (runs last) - frees compiled regexes after all code stops using them
697   // 2. Path cleanup (runs 3rd) - now a no-op but kept for API compatibility
698   // 3. Shared destroy (runs 2nd) - frees all shared resources
699   // 4. Cursor show (runs 1st) - shows cursor before exit
700   (void)atexit(asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all);
701   (void)atexit(path_cleanup_thread_locals);
702   (void)atexit(asciichat_shared_destroy);
703   (void)atexit(on_exit_show_cursor);
704 
705   // SECRET: Check for --backtrace (debug builds only) BEFORE options_init()
706   // Prints a backtrace and exits immediately - useful for debugging hangs
707 #ifndef NDEBUG
708   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
709     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0) {
710       log_info("=== Backtrace at startup ===");
711       backtrace_print_simple(0);
712       log_info("=== End Backtrace ===");
713       asciichat_shared_destroy();
714       return 0;
715     }
716   }
717 #endif
718 
719   // NOW parse all options - can use logging with colors!
720   asciichat_error_t options_result = options_init(argc, argv);
721   if (options_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
722     asciichat_error_context_t error_ctx;
723     if (HAS_ERRNO(&error_ctx)) {
724       log_error("Error: %s", error_ctx.context_message);
725     } else {
726       log_error("Error: Failed to initialize options (error code: %d)", options_result);
727     }
728 
729     // Clean up options state before exiting
730     options_state_destroy();
731     options_cleanup_schema();
732 
733     exit(options_result);
734   }
735 
736   // Get parsed options
737   const options_t *opts = options_get();
738   if (!opts) {
739     log_error("Error: Options not initialized");
740     return 1;
741   }
742 
743   // Determine final log file path (use mode-specific default from options if available)
744   // Determine output format early to decide on filename and logging strategy
745   bool use_json_logging = GET_OPTION(json);
746 
747   // Determine the log filename
748   // Check if user explicitly passed --log-file (not just the mode-specific default from options_init)
749   bool user_specified_log_file = false;
750   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
751     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0) {
752       user_specified_log_file = true;
753       break;
754     }
755   }
756 
757   const char *final_log_file = opts->log_file;
758   char json_filename_buf[256];
759 
760   if (use_json_logging) {
761     // When JSON logging is enabled, determine JSON output filename
762     if (user_specified_log_file) {
763       // User explicitly specified a log file - use it exactly for JSON output
764       SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
765     } else {
766       // Using default: replace .log with .json in the mode-specific default
767       // e.g., server.log -> server.json, mirror.log -> mirror.json, etc.
768       size_t len = strlen(final_log_file);
769       if (len > 4 && strcmp(&final_log_file[len - 4], ".log") == 0) {
770         // File ends with .log - replace with .json
771         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
772         // Replace .log with .json
773         strcpy(&json_filename_buf[len - 4], ".json");
774       } else {
775         // File doesn't end with .log - just append .json
776         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
777         strncat(json_filename_buf, ".json", sizeof(json_filename_buf) - strlen(json_filename_buf) - 1);
778       }
779     }
780     final_log_file = json_filename_buf;
781     // For JSON mode: Initialize logging without file (text will be disabled)
782     // We'll set up JSON output separately below
783     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
784   } else {
785     // Text logging mode: use the file from options (which is mode-specific default or user-specified)
786     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
787   }
788 
789   // Apply custom log template if specified (use early parsed value if available, otherwise use options)
790   const char *final_format = early_log_template ? early_log_template : GET_OPTION(log_template);
791   bool final_format_console_only =
792       early_log_template ? early_log_template_console_only : GET_OPTION(log_format_console_only);
793   if (final_format && final_format[0] != '\0') {
794     asciichat_error_t fmt_result = log_set_format(final_format, final_format_console_only);
795     if (fmt_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
796       log_error("Failed to apply custom log format");
797     }
798   }
799 
800   // Configure JSON output if requested
801   if (use_json_logging) {
802     // Open the JSON file for output
803     int json_fd = platform_open("main_log_file", final_log_file, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
804     if (json_fd >= 0) {
805       log_set_json_output(json_fd);
806     } else {
807       // Failed to open JSON file - report error and exit
808       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open JSON output file: %s", final_log_file);
809       return ERROR_CONFIG;
810     }
811   }
812 
813   // Initialize colors now that logging is fully initialized
814   // This must happen after log_init() since log_init_colors() checks if g_log.initialized
815   log_init_colors();
816 
817   // Apply quiet mode - disables terminal output
818   // Status screen mode only disables terminal output if terminal is interactive
819   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
820   if (GET_OPTION(quiet) ||
821       (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && GET_OPTION(status_screen) && terminal_is_interactive())) {
822     log_set_terminal_output(false);
823   }
824 
825   // Initialize palette based on command line options
826   const char *custom_chars = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : NULL;
827   if (apply_palette_config(GET_OPTION(palette_type), custom_chars) != 0) {
828     FATAL(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to apply palette configuration");
829   }
830 
831   // Set quiet mode for memory debugging
832 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
833   debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(GET_OPTION(quiet));
834 #endif
835 
836   // Truncate log if it's already too large
837   log_truncate_if_large();
838   fflush(stderr);
839 
840   // Handle --help and --version (these are detected and flagged by options_init)
841   // Terminal capabilities already initialized before options_init() at startup
842   if (opts->help) {
843     print_usage(opts->detected_mode);
844     fflush(NULL);
845     _Exit(0);
846   }
847 
848   if (opts->version) {
849     log_set_terminal_output(true);
850     action_show_version();
851     // action_show_version() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
852   }
853 
854 #ifndef NDEBUG
855   // Handle --debug-state (debug builds only)
856   // Sleep for specified time AFTER mode initialization so locks are created
857   if (IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT(debug_sync_state_time, opts) && opts->debug_sync_state_time > 0.0) {
858     log_info("Will print sync state after %f seconds", opts->debug_sync_state_time);
859   }
860 #endif
861 
862   // For server mode with status screen: disable terminal output only if interactive
863   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
864   // The status screen (when shown) will capture and display logs in its buffer instead
865   if (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && opts->status_screen && terminal_is_interactive()) {
866     log_set_terminal_output(false);
867   }
868 
869   log_dev("Logging initialized to %s", final_log_file);
870 
871   // Note: We do NOT auto-disable colors when stdout appears to be piped, because:
872   // 1. Tools like ripgrep can display ANSI colors when piped
873   // 2. Sandboxed/containerized environments may report false positives for isatty()
874   // 3. Users can explicitly disable colors with --color=false if needed
875   // Color behavior is now fully controlled by --color and --color-mode options.
876 
877 #ifndef NDEBUG
878   // Initialize lock debugging system after logging is fully set up
879   log_debug("Initializing lock debug system...");
880   int debug_sync_result = debug_sync_init();
881   if (debug_sync_result != 0) {
882     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync system initialization failed");
883     FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Debug sync system initialization failed");
884   }
885   log_debug("Debug sync system initialized successfully");
886 
887   // Initialize memory debug system
888   // TEMPORARILY DISABLED: these calls hang in non-interactive mode
889   // log_debug("Initializing memory debug system...");
890   // int debug_memory_result = debug_memory_thread_init();
891   // if (debug_memory_result != 0) {
892   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug system initialization failed");
893   //   FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Memory debug system initialization failed");
894   // }
895   // log_debug("Memory debug system initialized successfully");
896 
897   // Start memory debug (now just prints report directly)
898   // if (debug_memory_thread_start() != 0) {
899   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug startup failed");
900   //   FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Memory debug startup failed");
901   // }
902 
903 #ifndef _WIN32
904   // Unblock SIGUSR1 and SIGUSR2 at process level to ensure delivery
905   sigset_t set;
906   sigemptyset(&set);
907   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR1);
908   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR2);
909   sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, &set, NULL);
910 
911   // Register SIGUSR1 to trigger synchronization debugging output in all modes
912   signal_handler_t old_usr1 = platform_signal(SIGUSR1, common_handle_sigusr1);
913   if (old_usr1 == SIG_ERR) {
914     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR1 handler");
915   } else {
916     log_debug("SIGUSR1 handler registered successfully");
917   }
918 
919   // Register SIGUSR2 to trigger memory report in all modes
920   signal_handler_t old_usr2 = platform_signal(SIGUSR2, common_handle_sigusr2);
921   if (old_usr2 == SIG_ERR) {
922     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR2 handler");
923   } else {
924     log_debug("SIGUSR2 handler registered successfully");
925   }
926 #endif
927 #endif
928 
929   if (opts->fps > 0) {
930     if (opts->fps < 1 || opts->fps > 144) {
931       log_warn("FPS value %d out of range (1-144), using default", opts->fps);
932     } else {
933       log_debug("FPS set from command line: %d", opts->fps);
934     }
935   }
936 
937   // Automatic update check at startup (once per week maximum)
938   if (!GET_OPTION(no_check_update)) {
939     update_check_result_t update_result;
940     asciichat_error_t update_err = update_check_startup(&update_result);
941     if (update_err == ASCIICHAT_OK && update_result.update_available) {
942       char notification[1024];
943       update_check_format_notification(&update_result, notification, sizeof(notification));
944       log_info("%s", notification);
945 
946       // Set update notification for splash/status screens
947       splash_set_update_notification(notification);
948     }
949   }
950 
951   // Set up global signal handlers BEFORE mode dispatch
952   // All modes use the same centralized exit mechanism
953   setup_signal_handlers();
954 
955   // Register application callbacks so lib code can check exit flags
956   // This connects the render loop's should_exit check to signal_exit()
957   static const app_callbacks_t app_callbacks = {
958       .should_exit = should_exit,
959       .signal_exit = signal_exit,
960   };
961   app_callbacks_register(&app_callbacks);
962 
963   // Register shutdown callback so splash thread and other code can check for exit
964   shutdown_register_callback((shutdown_check_fn)should_exit);
965 
966 #ifndef NDEBUG
967   // Start debug threads now, after initialization but before mode entry
968   // This avoids lock contention during critical initialization phase
969   // Note: debug_sync_start_thread() and debug_memory_thread_start() now use
970   // direct pthread calls instead of mutex_init/cond_init to avoid named registry
971   // deadlock during thread startup
972   if (debug_sync_start_thread() != 0) {
973     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync thread startup failed");
974     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Debug sync thread startup failed");
975   }
976   log_debug("Debug sync thread started");
977 
978 #endif
979 
980   // Find and dispatch to mode entry point
981   const mode_descriptor_t *mode = find_mode(opts->detected_mode);
982   if (!mode) {
983     log_error("Error: Mode not found for detected_mode=%d", opts->detected_mode);
984     return 1;
985   }
986 
987   // Call the mode-specific entry point
988   // Mode entry points use options_get() to access parsed options
989   int exit_code = mode->entry_point();
990 
991   // Named registry cleanup is handled by asciichat_shared_destroy()
992   // (called via atexit handler), which runs AFTER the memory report
993 
994   if (exit_code == ERROR_USAGE) {
995     exit(ERROR_USAGE);
996   }
997 
998   return exit_code;
999 }






References action_show_version(),
    app_callbacks_register(), apply_color_mode_override(),
    apply_palette_config(), ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all(),
    asciichat_shared_destroy(), asciichat_shared_init(),
    asciichat_thread_self(), backtrace_print_simple(),
    colorscheme_load_builtin(),
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    debug_atomic_init(), debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(),
    debug_sync_init(), debug_sync_set_main_thread_id(),
    debug_sync_start_thread(),
    options_state::debug_sync_state_time,
    detect_terminal_capabilities(), options_state::detected_mode,
    mode_descriptor_t::entry_point, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, ERROR_THREAD, ERROR_USAGE,
    FATAL, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE, options_state::fps,
    g_argc, g_argv, g_color_flag_passed,
    g_color_flag_value, GET_OPTION, grep_init(),
    HAS_ERRNO, options_state::help, IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT,
    log_debug, log_dev, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    log_file, options_state::log_file, log_info,
    log_init_colors(), log_redetect_terminal_capabilities(),
    log_set_color_scheme(), log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_format(), log_set_json_output(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_truncate_if_large(),
    log_warn, MODE_CLIENT, MODE_DISCOVERY,
    MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, MODE_INVALID, MODE_MIRROR,
    MODE_SERVER, named_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD,
    named_registry_register_packet_types(),
    options_cleanup_schema(), options_colorscheme_init_early(),
    options_get(), options_init(), options_state_destroy(),
    options_state::palette_custom,
    options_state::palette_custom_set,
    path_cleanup_thread_locals(), platform_open(),
    platform_signal(), REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, SET_ERRNO, setup_signal_handlers(),
    should_exit(), shutdown_register_callback(),
    signal_exit(), splash_set_update_notification(),
    options_state::status_screen, terminal_cursor_show(),
    terminal_is_interactive(), terminal_should_force_stderr(),
    update_check_result_t::update_available,
    update_check_format_notification(), update_check_startup(),
    and options_state::version.






void
  set_interrupt_callback (void(* cb )(void))


Register a mode-specific interrupt callback




Called by mode-specific code to register a callback that will be
    invoked when signal_exit() is called. Used to shut down network
    sockets so threads blocked in recv() unblock quickly instead of waiting for
    timeouts.




The callback is called synchronously from signal_exit(), so
    it must be async-signal-safe (only atomics, socket_shutdown(), no
    malloc/etc).




Only one callback can be registered at a time. Setting a new
    callback replaces the previous one.




Parameters


cb Function to call on exit signal, or NULL to
  unregister


Definition at line 218 of file main.c.


218                                               {
219   g_interrupt_callback = cb;
220 }










void
  setup_signal_handlers (void )


Set up global signal handlers Called once at startup before mode
    dispatch


Definition at line 337 of file main.c.


337                                  {
338   platform_set_console_ctrl_handler(console_ctrl_handler);
339 
340 #ifndef _WIN32
341   platform_signal_handler_t handlers[] = {
342       {SIGINT, handle_sigterm},
343       {SIGTERM, handle_sigterm},
344       {SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN},
345   };
346   platform_register_signal_handlers(handlers, 3);
347   log_debug("Signal handlers registered");
348 #endif
349 }






References log_debug,
    platform_register_signal_handlers(), and
    platform_set_console_ctrl_handler().


Referenced by main().






bool should_exit (void )


Check if the application should exit




Called by mode main loops to detect shutdown requests from signals
    or errors.




Returns


true if shutdown has been requested, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 202 of file main.c.


202                        {
203   return atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
204 }






References atomic_load_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.






void
  signal_exit (void )


Signal that the application should exit




Sets the global exit flag and calls the registered interrupt
    callback if set. Safe to call from signal handlers (uses only atomics and
    function pointers). Called by signal handlers (SIGTERM, Ctrl+C) and normal
    code (errors, timeouts).


Definition at line 206 of file main.c.


206                        {
207   // Note: This function may be called from a signal handler context where
208   // other threads may hold mutex locks. We avoid log_console() here to prevent
209   // deadlock (render loop holds terminal lock, signal handler can't acquire it).
210   // The shutdown will be logged by normal thread context later.
211   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
212   void (*cb)(void) = g_interrupt_callback;
213   if (cb) {
214     cb();
215   }
216 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.13
    ascii-chat
